# @fluenti/react

> React bindings for Fluenti — compile-time i18n with zero runtime overhead.

## Overview

@fluenti/react provides React components, hooks, and a context provider for internationalization. It supports both client-side React and React Server Components (RSC). Built on @fluenti/core.

- Package: @fluenti/react
- Docs: https://fluenti.dev
- Repository: https://github.com/usefluenti/fluenti/tree/main/packages/react
- Peer dependency: react >=18.0.0
- License: MIT

## Exports

Main entry (`@fluenti/react`):
- I18nProvider: Context provider wrapping the component tree
- useI18n(): Hook returning {i18n, locale, setLocale, isLoading, loadedLocales, preloadLocale}
- Trans: Rich text with component interpolation
- Plural: Locale-aware pluralization via Intl.PluralRules
- Select: Categorical value selection (gender, role, etc.)
- DateTime: Date formatting via Intl.DateTimeFormat
- NumberFormat: Number formatting via Intl.NumberFormat
- msg: Lazy message descriptor for use outside the component tree
- I18nContext: Raw React context (advanced use)

Server entry (`@fluenti/react/server`):
- createServerI18n(config): Create request-scoped i18n utilities for RSC
  - Returns: {setLocale, getI18n, Trans, Plural, DateTime, NumberFormat}
  - Uses React.cache() for per-request isolation
- SSR utilities re-exported from @fluenti/core: detectLocale, getSSRLocaleScript, getHydratedLocale, isRTL, getDirection

## Quick Start

```tsx
import { I18nProvider, useI18n, Trans, Plural } from '@fluenti/react'

function App() {
  return (
    <I18nProvider locale="en" fallbackLocale="en" messages={{ en, zhCN }}>
      <MyApp />
    </I18nProvider>
  )
}

function Home() {
  const { i18n, locale, setLocale } = useI18n()
  return <h1>{i18n.t('Hello, {name}!', { name: 'World' })}</h1>
}
```

## Server Components (RSC)

```ts
import { createServerI18n } from '@fluenti/react/server'

export const { setLocale, getI18n, Trans, Plural, DateTime, NumberFormat } = createServerI18n({
  loadMessages: (locale) => import(`../locales/compiled/${locale}.ts`),
  fallbackLocale: 'en',
  resolveLocale: async () => {
    const { cookies } = await import('next/headers')
    return (await cookies()).get('locale')?.value ?? 'en'
  },
})
```
